page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc9i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 96 physical Intel Xeon 6982P-C cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. This architecture ensures that each virtual core has exclusive access to a physical execution unit, eliminating resource contention from hyperthreading.

The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, requiring reliance on external network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 48 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers for distributed workloads.

This hardware profile is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require high single-core performance and predictable execution times. Suitable applications include high-performance computing (HPC), batch processing, scientific modeling, and large-scale web serving where CPU-bound tasks dominate and local storage is not a primary requirement.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc9i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides 96 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring dedicated execution resources without hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 48 Gbps. This configuration is engineered for high-performance computing, intensive batch processing, and CPU-bound workloads requiring consistent, dedicated processing power.

tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized instance featuring ninety-six dedicated physical cores and high-speed network bandwidth for demanding CPU-bound workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
